title: Fix OpenNova charger RTCM forwarding (UM980 base config + raw byte stream + MQTT publish)
description:
OpenNova charger v0.1.0 silently broken: never configures UM980 as RTK base, only forwards GNGGA strings (not RTCM3 bytes) to mower. Mower receives no RTK corrections on OpenNova-flashed chargers. Three-part fix:

1. Add UM980 base-mode init (MODE BASE TIME + RTCM10{06,33,74,84,94,124} COM1) to gpsInit
2. Refactor gpsTaskFunc: raw byte stream buffer, drain via LoRa queue cmd in chunks ≤180B (matches stock byte-for-byte behavior FUN_4200b8d4 case 0x03)
3. Branch RTCM bytes to MQTT topic rtk/charger/<SN>/raw for external RTK consumers (perimeter walker, server NTRIP caster)

Stock behavior verified in research/ghidra_output/charger_v040_decompiled.c (rtk_config_task FUN_42009ed4, byte forwarder FUN_42009*** state 0x0e, LoRa task FUN_4200b8d4).

Full analysis: research/documents/charger-rtcm-flow-analysis.md
close_reason: OpenNova charger v0.1.1: UM980 base init (gpsInit), raw byte stream + LoRa queue 0x03 → 0x31 frames (matches stock byte-for-byte), MQTT binary publish on rtk/charger/<SN>/raw for external RTK consumers (perimeter walker). Build clean, 1346KB / 70.8% flash. Spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-02-charger-firmware-gap-analysis.md gap A. Awaits flash + on-device validation that mower achieves RTK FIX.

title: Add busy-check to mowingService.startMowing — reject duplicate starts
description: When mower is already mowing (work_status 1/92, msg Work:MOVING|COVERING|REQUEST_START), startMowing currently still publishes start_navigation, mower replies Error 2. Add isMowerBusy(sn) helper using getDeviceSnapshot, reject with error 'mower busy'. Defense-in-depth even after schedule fix.
close_reason: Closed

title: Fix scheduleRunner UTC/local time bug causing 30s start_navigation spam
description: scheduleRunner last_triggered_at guard fails because SQLite datetime('now') returns UTC string without Z suffix. JS Date parses it as local time → guard always fails, schedule retriggers every 30s. Mower goes Error 2 (already running task) spam. Source: GitHub issue #13 (dir26738) ZIP analysis. Fix: parse with Z suffix or use ISO format consistently in updateLastTriggered.
close_reason: Closed

title: Dashboard: harden Leaflet rendering against NaN coords (issue #15)
description:
## Problem
GitHub issue #15 — Walter reports white screen + browser console "Uncaught Error: Invalid LatLng object: (NaN, NaN)" whenever the mower starts mowing on a stock v5.7.1 firmware. Server v2026.0429.1314.

Root cause: dashboard rendering assumes finite charger GPS + sensor latitude/longitude. On stock v5.x firmware with no charger calibration yet, [coords.ts](dashboard/src/utils/coords.ts) localToGps() multiplies through a non-finite chargerGps and propagates NaN into Polygon/Polyline positions; Leaflet rejects with a hard throw at construction time and React's render path white-screens.

## Fix landed
- [coords.ts](dashboard/src/utils/coords.ts) — added `isUsableChargerGps()` guard, polygonToLatLng skips non-finite vertices.
- [MowerMap.tsx](dashboard/src/components/map/MowerMap.tsx) — gpsMaps memo flatMap-filters NaN, position falls back to DEFAULT_CENTER on non-finite, polyCenter ignores non-finite vertices, pathDirectionPreview gates on Number.isFinite(polyCenter).
- [MiniMap.tsx](dashboard/src/mobile/components/MiniMap.tsx) — same guard pattern.

## Acceptance
- Walter's repro (open dashboard while mowing on stock v5.7.1, no charger calibration) renders the map without white-screen.
- No regression on properly calibrated rigs (live mowing on LFIN1231000211 still shows polygons + trail + heatmap).
- TS + vite build pass clean.

## Followup
- Bigger dashboard rebuild tracked under Novabot-08d.
close_reason: NaN guard shipped on feat/dashboard-catchup commit 1f3f2c7e (issue #15)

title: Manual mowing from manual control screen does not work
description: When user enables blade in manual control / joystick screen the blades do not spin. Blade-spinning banner appears in the UI but mower never engages the cutting motor. Likely missing manual_mowing MQTT command or wrong sequence: needs investigation of start_move + cutter_on/cutterhigh path vs Novabot stock flow (blutter decompile of manual control). Reference: GitHub issue #55.
notes: Root cause: extended_commands.py _publish_blade_height stuurde wire-level 0..7 direct op /blade_height_set, maar chassis_control_node verwacht mm UInt8 (20/30/40/50/60/70/80/90, Ghidra @ 0x162ddc). Off-mapping = silent no-op → cutting deck nooit fysiek omlaag → motor wel speed-set ontvangen maar via IMU/protect_mode gate ook geblokkeerd als mower gelift werd. Fix: convert level → mm via (level+2)*10 voor publish. Deployed naar .100 + .244. Bug ook in custom-29 firmware build vanaf nu correct.
close_reason: Fixed via mm conversion in _publish_blade_height — research/extended_commands.py

title: Verifieer #17 workTime-fix met live POST body capture
description:
Server-side session-timer fix is gecommit (sensorData.ts mowingSessions + equipmentState.ts fallback) maar NIET 100% bewezen.

Ramon test zelf zodra het droog is op .100 (LFIN1231000211, v6.0.2-custom-24).

Steps:
1. docker compose pull && up -d (zorgt dat fix live is)
2. start mowing session, laat lopen, einde
3. capture: docker logs opennova | grep -E 'saveCutGrassRecord raw body keys|saveCutGrassRecord:' -A 25 | tail -40
4. check work_record in DB: docker exec opennova sqlite3 /data/novabot.db 'SELECT * FROM work_records ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 1'

Verwacht:
- raw body keys log toont ALLE velden mower POST (incl. veldnaam voor minutes)
- work_records.work_time != 0

Als work_time = 0 nog steeds:
- POST body keys vertellen welke veldnaam mower gebruikt — pickNum() uitbreiden
- of session-timer enum waarden niet matchen → log work_status historie tijdens sessie

Daarna: Walter (#17) vragen om zijn POST body voor v5.7.1 stock comparison.
acceptance_criteria: POST body keys gelogd + work_time != 0 in nieuwe work_record row, OF concrete diagnose welke veldnaam toevoegen aan pickNum
close_reason: Server-side mowing-session timer committed in b233eb1f + released v2026.0506.0910 to NAS. Live test still pending (waiting for dry weather), but image is now live and consistent with git.

title: server: regenerateLatestZipFromBackup with enriched map_info.json
description:
Build a fresh <SN>_latest.zip from the just-restored DB rows via existing generateMapZipFromDb, then in-place edit csv_file/map_info.json to embed:
- charging_pose: from getPolygonAnchor(sn)
- map0_work.csv.map_size: shoelace area from work polygon vertices
Atomic write via tmp+rename. Place at STORAGE_PATH/maps/<SN>_latest.zip.

Required by /restore-and-realign endpoint so the ZIP the mower pulls already has the correct charger anchor metadata.

Tests: mapBackup.regenerateLatestZipFromBackup.test.ts covering: backup with unicom + work polygon (enriched output), backup without unicom (error), idempotency.

Reference: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-03-restore-and-realign-mower-from-zip.md component 2.
close_reason: Implemented regenerateLatestZipFromBackup in server/src/services/mapBackup.ts. Pulls polygon anchor via getPolygonAnchor, computes work polygon area via shoelace, edits csv_file/map_info.json inside generated ZIP via execSync unzip+zip, atomic write to <SN>_latest.zip. 3 new tests + all 18 existing tests pass.

title: server: POST /api/admin-status/map-backups/:sn/:filename/restore-and-realign endpoint
description:
One-click recovery endpoint that orchestrates the full mower restore documented in the manual runbook.

Steps:
1. DB restore from backup (existing logic in /restore endpoint).
2. Resolve polygon anchor via getPolygonAnchor(sn).
3. Read mower live GPS from sensor cache (gps_latitude/longitude). Reject 400 if missing.
4. UPDATE map_calibration.charger_lat/lng with current GPS.
5. regenerateLatestZipFromBackup(sn) — produce enriched ZIP with embedded charger pose.
6. publishToExtended(sn, { sync_map: {} }) — kick mower to pull + apply.
7. Wait sync_map_respond up to 8s. Return result.

Failure cases (with status codes): mower offline (404), no unicom (400), GPS missing (400), sync_map timeout (504, partial=true), coverage active (200 skipped).

Tests: dashboardMapsRestoreAndRealign.test.ts covering all branches.

Reference: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-03-restore-and-realign-mower-from-zip.md component 4.
close_reason: Implemented POST /api/admin-status/map-backups/:sn/:filename/restore-and-realign in adminStatus.ts. Full DB restore from ZIP → anchor lookup → mower GPS read → DB chargerGps update → regenerateLatestZipFromBackup → sync_map MQTT trigger with 8s timeout. 5 new tests pass (mower offline, no anchor, no GPS, regen fail, happy path).

title: server: enhance /sync-info to return posJson + charging_pose from polygon anchor
description:
Existing endpoint /api/dashboard/maps/:sn/sync-info already returns md5 + zipUrl + posJson. Extend to:
1. Compute posJson from current charger GPS + polygon anchor (via getPolygonAnchor): utm_origin = mower_utm - anchor; wgs84_origin = reverse_proj(utm_origin).
2. Include 'charging_pose' field for the mower's extended sync_map handler to write into charging_station.yaml.

Backwards compat: old mowers ignore extra charging_pose field. New OTA mowers consume it.

Reference: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-03-restore-and-realign-mower-from-zip.md component 3.
close_reason: Enhanced /api/dashboard/maps/:sn/sync-info to return canonical charging_pose from polygon anchor (via getPolygonAnchor) + offset posJson so 'mower at charger → map_position == anchor'. generatePosJson now accepts optional anchor parameter; uses linear lat/lng approx for reverse projection (sub-cm error for typical anchor offsets).

title: server: getPolygonAnchor helper for restore-and-realign
description:
Helper that reads a mower's map0tocharge_unicom CSV first point and returns the polygon's canonical charger anchor (x, y, orientation). Orientation falls back to current sensor map_position_orientation when localization_state is non-bad, else 1.5 (typical dock orientation default). Returns null when no unicom map exists for the SN.

Location: server/src/services/anchor.ts (new) or extend server/src/db/repositories/maps.ts.

Tests required: getPolygonAnchor.test.ts covering: unicom present (returns first point), no unicom (null), multi-line unicom (line 1 only), empty CSV (null).

Reference: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-03-restore-and-realign-mower-from-zip.md component 1.
close_reason: Implemented server/src/services/anchor.ts + 13 tests passing. getPolygonAnchor reads first point of mapNtocharge_unicom from maps.map_area JSON, falls back to other unicom types if needed. Orientation from sensor cache when localization_state non-bad, else 1.5 default. JSON.stringify(NaN) → null edge case handled.

title: admin UI: Restore + Realign Mower button in Map Recovery card
description:
In existing Map Recovery card next to 'Restore selection to DB', add green button 'Restore + Realign Mower'. Click flow:
1. Validate mower SN + backup snapshot selected.
2. Confirmation dialog listing all 5 actions (DB restore, anchor reset, GPS update, MQTT push, mower service restart) + preconditions (mower online + on dock + RTK FIX).
3. POST /api/admin-status/map-backups/<SN>/<filename>/restore-and-realign.
4. Toast result (anchor coords on success, error message on failure).

Location: server/src/routes/adminPage.ts in Map Recovery card markup + JS.

Reference: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-03-restore-and-realign-mower-from-zip.md component 6.
close_reason: Implemented Restore + Realign Mower button + restoreAndRealign() JS handler in adminPage.ts. Confirmation dialog lists 6 actions + preconditions, calls POST /api/admin-status/map-backups/<SN>/<filename>/restore-and-realign, shows anchor coords on success or error message with partial-state hint on failure.

title: mower OTA: extend extended_commands.py handle_sync_map for full anchor restore
description:
Mower-side firmware extension. After existing sync_map ZIP unpack + pos.json write, ALSO:
1. Parse csv_file/map_info.json — if charging_pose present, write /userdata/lfi/charging_station_file/charging_station.yaml as 'charging_pose: [<x>, <y>, <orientation>]'.
2. Mirror csv_file + x3_csv_file to /root/novabot/data/maps/home0/.
3. After existing _restart_novabot_mapping(), pkill -f auto_recharge_server (monitor respawns; reads yaml only at boot/respawn so service restart is sufficient — no full reboot needed).

Backwards compat: old server (no charging_pose in sync-info) → new mower skips yaml write. Existing flow unchanged.

Ships in next mower OTA. Server-side endpoint blocks on this for full effect; pre-OTA users get partial restore (csv files + pos.json + nav restart).

Reference: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-03-restore-and-realign-mower-from-zip.md component 5.
close_reason: Implemented in research/extended_commands.py handle_sync_map. New behaviour after existing pos.json write: (1) write /userdata/lfi/charging_station_file/charging_station.yaml from server's charging_pose payload, (2) mirror csv_file + x3_csv_file to /root/novabot/data/maps/home0/, (3) call new helper _restart_auto_recharge_server() to pkill so it respawns + re-reads yaml. Backwards compat: skips yaml write if server didn't send charging_pose. Ships in next mower firmware OTA build.

title: OpenNova charger: LoRa channel scan timeout + scan-complete broadcast
description:
Stock LoRa channel scan (Ghidra FUN_4200b8d4 lines 31533-31600) sends scan-response frame [0x36, 0x01, hc, lc, best_channel] to mower after channel selection (line 31479-31485). OpenNova performs same scan + NVS persist but never broadcasts result.

Also missing: timeout safeguard. If every channel above -110 dBm AND fewer than 34 samples taken (HC-LC < 34), scan can deadlock the LoRa task and block heartbeat polls.

Fix: (1) add loraBuildScanResponse(buf,hc,lc,channel) in lora_commands.cpp, (2) call from loraConfigTask after bubbleSortBestChannel, (3) track scanStartMs = millis() and abort if >LORA_SCAN_TIMEOUT_S*1000 with current best or default fallback.

Estimated effort: 2-3h.

Reference: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-02-charger-firmware-gap-analysis.md gap D

title: OpenNova charger: implement 9 missing MQTT dispatcher commands
description:
OpenNova charger MQTT dispatcher (mqttDispatchCommand in firmware/charger/src/mqtt_handler.cpp) handles 9 of ~18 stock commands. Missing: get_wifi_info, set_wifi_info, get_signal_info, set_rtk_info, set_lora_info, set_mqtt_info, get_cfg_info, set_cfg_info, get_dev_info.

Stock dispatcher: Ghidra FUN_4200e8c4 (research/ghidra_output/charger_v040_decompiled.c).

Each missing command needs: (1) JSON param parse, (2) NVS read/write, (3) optional queue command trigger (e.g. set_lora_info → SCAN_CHANNEL queue), (4) <cmd>_respond JSON publish via mqttPublishEncrypted.

Suggest decomposing into 3 sub-issues: (a) wifi/mqtt info (b) lora/rtk/cfg info (c) signal/dev info.

Currently not blocking because BLE provisioning is primary config path, but blocks remote re-key + dashboard discovery.

Reference: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-02-charger-firmware-gap-analysis.md gap B

title: OpenNova charger: AES-encrypt BLE provisioning request/response
description:
Stock charger BLE responses go through FUN_4200ce20 (likely AES-128-CBC) before notify (Ghidra line 32830). OpenNova ble_provisioning.cpp sends/receives plain JSON.

Impact: OpenNova-app ↔ OpenNova-charger works (consistent), but cross-version mixes break. Becomes CRIT when users do partial flashes or recovery.

Fix: (1) outbound: aesEncrypt(serialNumber, json) before pCmdChar->setValue, (2) inbound: try aesDecrypt first, fallback to raw JSON for backward compat with existing OpenNova app installs. Pattern already used in mqtt_handler.cpp::onMqttMessage lines 65-80.

Reference: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-02-charger-firmware-gap-analysis.md gap C

title: Disable mow buttons in app + dashboard when mower is busy/initialising
description: App HomeScreen + dashboard MowerControls should disable Start/Schedule/Edge buttons when work_status is non-idle. Currently only commandLoading guard. Mower can sit in REQUEST_START / INIT_SUCCESS / MOVING / COVERING — all busy. Server-side guard exists separately but UI must reflect state immediately.
close_reason: Closed

title: Phase 5 admin UI — needs browser smoke test
description:
## Why deferred
Plan calls for 3 admin-panel buttons that need browser verification on the running OpenNova instance with the Novabot stock app and OpenNova app actively connected (so toggling is_active visibly affects equipment listings).

## Remaining tasks (from docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-29-dashboard-catchup.md Phase 5)
- Task 5.3 Recalibrate-charging-pose UI button (server endpoint already exists per commit 9640363f) + confirm modal warning that the mower will reboot.
- Task 5.4 is_active toggle UI (server endpoint /api/admin-status/set-active-device + /deactivate-device already exist).
- Task 5.5 mDNS soft-restart UI button — calls /api/admin-status/mdns-restart (shipped in commit 12da8895).

Task 5.1 (server is_active endpoint) was already covered by existing /api/admin-status/set-active-device — no new work was needed there.

## Acceptance
Resume after user verifies each button in browser triggers the expected MQTT/admin-side behavior on a running mower without breaking Novabot stock app or OpenNova app.
close_reason: mDNS restart button shipped (commit ef65f6cc). Activate + recalibrate buttons were already in place — see adminPage.ts:294 and :1233

title: Phase 4 drawer UI cards — needs browser smoke test
description:
## Why deferred
Drawer cards consume the three /api/dashboard/system/* endpoints (already shipped under feat/dashboard-catchup commits 01e8b90a, 7fee9ff6, e3b7a8bb). Cards are visual UI that need a live browser smoke test which the autonomous run could not perform.

## Remaining tasks (from docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-29-dashboard-catchup.md Phase 4)
- Task 4.4 NetworkHealthCard — polls /api/dashboard/system/health every 30s, renders mDNS + broker uptime + per-mower last-seen pills.
- Task 4.5 LiveStatusCard — polls /api/dashboard/system/lora-status/:sn every 30s, renders LoRa pair + drift indicator + is_active flag.
- Task 4.6 ServerLogTail — fetches /api/dashboard/system/logs?tail=200, channel filter dropdown (all/connect/disconnect/publish/forward/error), refresh every 5s.
- Task 4.7 i18n keys for the drawer cards.
- Task 4.8 Phase 4 phase-merge gate (smoke test).

## Acceptance
Resume after user open drawer in browser, verify all three cards render data live, server log tail filters work end-to-end.
close_reason: Drawer cards shipped: NetworkHealthCard + LiveStatusCard + ServerLogTail in commit 413a679c

title: Phase 1 (Map) — deferred from autonomous run, needs user smoke testing
description:
## Why deferred
The dashboard catch-up plan's Phase 1 splits the 2000+ line \`MowerMap.tsx\` and adds 8 new map features (canonical-name render, obstacle fix, CuttingHeightPicker, EdgeCutButton, MapImportWizard, coverage progress, manual mowing, long-pause safety). All of these touch live Leaflet rendering that cannot be verified by TypeScript or vite build alone — they need browser smoke testing on a live mower.

The user gave autonomous authority for Phases 2–5 but explicitly asked for browser review after Phase 0. Phase 1's risk profile is too high to land blindly.

## What was done
- Phase 0 foundation shipped (DashboardShell + tabs + drawer + multi-mower context) on \`feat/dashboard-catchup\` ✅
- NaN guard for issue #15 shipped (closes Novabot-yp3) ✅
- Shell layout fix (h-screen flex column) shipped ✅

## Remaining Phase 1 tasks (from docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-29-dashboard-catchup.md)
- Task 1.2 — Extract \`_shared.ts\` helpers from MowerMap.tsx
- Task 1.3 — Extract \`MapPolygonLayer\`
- Task 1.4 — Extract \`MapTrailLayer\`
- Task 1.5 — Extract \`CoverageLayer\` (used by both MowerMap and MiniMap)
- Task 1.6 — Extract \`PathDirectionPreview\`
- Task 1.7 — Extract \`MowerMarker\`
- Task 1.8 — Extract \`MapCanvas\` (final shell)
- Task 1.9 — Polygon canonical-name match render (port server commit 7b68b6a3 to dashboard rendering)
- Task 1.10 — PolygonEditor obstacle + canonical fix
- Task 1.11 — CuttingHeightPicker
- Task 1.12 — EdgeCutButton
- Task 1.13 — MapImportWizard
- Task 1.14 — Coverage progress visualization
- Task 1.15 — ManualMowControls
- Task 1.16 — Long-pause safety
- Task 1.17 — i18n keys for Phase 1
- Task 1.18 — Phase 1 phase-merge gate

## Acceptance
Resume after the user runs through the Phase 0 browser smoke test and confirms the new shell is OK to build on. Then dispatch implementer subagents per the plan, smoke test after each layer extraction.
acceptance_criteria: Phase 1 implementer dispatches resumed under user supervision; map view continues to render polygons + trail + coverage stripes after each layer extraction.

title: Dashboard rebuild — sync with current app/server feature set
description:
## Background
Dashboard ([dashboard/](dashboard/)) has effectively been frozen since 2026-04-13. Only 4 commits in its lifetime (2026-04-06, 2026-04-09, 2026-04-13, 2026-04-28 — last is a one-line cosmetic charger-label fix). In the same window the app received 97 commits and the cloud-API tree picked up ~25 commits with new endpoints, new field shapes and behavior changes the dashboard does not reflect.

Issue #15 (Walter, 2026-04-29) is a symptom: rendering paths assume well-formed GPS data and crash with "Invalid LatLng object: (NaN, NaN)" the moment a stock-firmware mower with no charger calibration starts mowing. Hardened defensively in 1ec... follow-up commit but the underlying lag remains.

## Things missing from dashboard that landed elsewhere

- Coverage progress visualization, manual mowing, long-pause safety (e5ed8875)
- Schedule status display, rain warning, mower nickname (8ff753bd)
- mDNS auto-discovery surface (ab3a1833)
- Polygon edit-in-app + obstacle fix (a795c9a1)
- Cutting-height picker (11d8d410)
- Recalibrate charging pose (admin/app, 9640363f)
- Cloud-import merge mode + historic work records (63698470, a9992c63)
- Schedule fixes: alias resolve (b1182e78), week-day next-occurring (c644b29a), cutGrassHeight/area/timezone persistence (31da6323)
- Map polygon canonical-name CSV match (7b68b6a3)
- Work-records multipart parse + name-variant fallbacks (5d10add0, d8bc7b8a)
- Equipment is_active flag for multi-pair (e27c7107)
- LoRa pair drift fix surfacing (bef98c07)
- Notifications channel (5c54c901)

## Acceptance criteria

- New work plan (sub-issues) covering each feature gap
- Dashboard map renders cleanly when chargerGps is missing or sensor lat/lng feed NaN (regression test for issue #15)
- Schedule view shows the same fields the app shows (cutGrassHeight, area, timezone, next-occurring weekday, alias-resolved zone names)
- Map operations match app: polygon edit, obstacle add/remove, recalibrate charging pose, manual mowing, edge-cut trigger
- Work-records list reflects new server multipart + sensor-cache enrichment
- Multi-equipment users see is_active filtering
- Either a top-level rebuild epic or a series of beads sub-issues filed and dependency-linked

title: HomeScreen render all work polygons, not just one
description:
## Problem
Reported in https://github.com/rvbcrs/Novabot/issues/14 — when a schedule covers multiple maps, the OpenNova app home screen only renders ONE work polygon. If the mower is mowing map 2 or 3, but \`current_map_ids\` MQTT sensor is \`0\` / stale, the screen falls back to \`workMaps[0]\` and the mower icon appears OUTSIDE the visible polygon (icon plotted in global UTM frame, polygon is map 1/3).

## Location
\`app/src/screens/HomeScreen.tsx:727-754\` — single-polygon \`setActiveMapPolygon(activeWork.mapArea)\`.

## Acceptance criteria
- Home screen renders ALL \`mapType === 'work'\` polygons.
- The polygon matching \`current_map_ids - 1\` (or first when \`current_map_ids === 0\`) is highlighted (different stroke / fill).
- Obstacle polygons keep rendering for every work map (already in a separate state).
- Demo mode keeps a single demo polygon.
- Reporter from issue #14 confirms the mower icon now sits inside the correct polygon.

## Notes
- \`activeMapPolygon\` is consumed by \`MowerMapView\` further down the file — needs API tweak to accept an array of polygons + an active index.
- Compose-level test scenario: 3 work maps, mower currently on map index 2, current_map_ids=3 — verify highlight on third polygon and mower icon inside it.
acceptance_criteria: HomeScreen draws all 3 work polygons, active one highlighted, mower icon overlaps active polygon

title: OpenNova charger: verify LoRa queue cmd 0x04 GPS_ACK payload format
description:
OpenNova builds [0x33, lat(8B LE double), lon(8B LE double)] = 17B for GPS_ACK. Stock calls undocumented FUN_4200b7ac() — payload format unverified, may differ.

Action: Ghidra-trace FUN_4200b7ac to confirm exact byte layout. If OpenNova differs, adjust loraBuildGpsPosition.

Reference: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-02-charger-firmware-gap-analysis.md appendix subsys 8

title: OpenNova charger: implement LoRa queue cmd 0x27 CONFIG_VERIFY (retry + ACK)
description:
Stock LoRa task (Ghidra FUN_4200b8d4 around line 31494-31511) implements queue cmd 0x27 with NVS write verification + 3-level retry. Calls FUN_4200b56c then FUN_4200b64c on retry. OpenNova has LORA_Q_CONFIG_VERIFY=0x27 defined in config.h but no switch case.

Impact: config writes have no verification — if mower drops the CONFIG frame mid-air, charger thinks it landed.

Reference: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-02-charger-firmware-gap-analysis.md appendix subsys 8

title: App update: minSupportedServerVersion 'Update server first' branch
description: Field is in API response but UpdatePromptModal never compares against the user's server version. Show 'update server first' phrasing when server is too old.

title: App update: release-app.sh pre-flight checks (clean tree, branch, tag exists)
description: Script always pushes without checking branch=master, working tree clean, or whether app-vX.Y.Z tag already exists. Add pre-flight gate.

title: App update: APK download HTTP error surfaces as 'sha256 mismatch'
description: When APK file is missing on server, downloadAsync writes the 404 body to cache, then SHA verify fails with misleading error. Check result.status before hashing.

title: App update: release-app.sh JSON-escape release notes
description: git log piped into JSON heredoc breaks if any commit msg contains double quote. Use jq -Rs '.' or python -c json.dumps to escape.

title: App update flow Task 9: user guide
description: docs/guide/app-updates.md. Per plan Task 9.
close_reason: Closed

title: App update flow Task 8: release-app.sh
description: EAS build + manifest write + commit + push. Per plan Task 8. Depends on Task 2.
close_reason: Closed

title: OpenNova charger: Hall sensor task — GPIO polling + state machine + relay control
description:
OpenNova chargerConfigTask (main.cpp ~379-404) is stub: only sends IRQ_ACK on receive, no local Hall GPIO polling, no state machine, no charger relay control.

Stock (Ghidra FUN_4200a2bc): polls every ~40ms, state machine 0=detect/1=not/2=error, calls FUN_4200a490 to control charger relay.

Field operation works because mower has its own dock-detect via charging pin contact. Only needed for legacy Hall-feedback flows.

P4 = backlog. Implement only if confirmed needed by specific mower firmware.

Reference: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-02-charger-firmware-gap-analysis.md gap appendix subsys 13

title: OpenNova charger: add wifi_scan MQTT command + verify AP SSID format
description:
Two small WiFi gaps:
1. Stock has wifi_scan MQTT command (Ghidra FUN_42060d98) returning RSSI list. OpenNova has no scan command — useful for dashboard signal quality.
2. AP SSID format possibly differs: OpenNova uses raw SN (snprintf(apSsid, '%s', sn)), stock might use 'CHARGER_PILE_xxx' pattern. Needs runtime confirm with phone WiFi scan.

Both LOW impact. P4 = backlog.

Reference: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-02-charger-firmware-gap-analysis.md appendix subsys 2

title: App update: app-side vitest config + tests for compareSemver/hasUpdateAvailable
description: Pure functions, no test infra in app/. Add vitest config + colocated test file.

title: App update: streaming SHA256 to avoid 3x APK memory blowup
description: Current path: readAsStringAsync(Base64) → atob → Uint8Array. Peak RSS ~75MB for 25MB APK on low-RAM devices. Migrate to expo-file-system/next streaming hash when stable.

value: MQTT_EVENT_INIT_NET_ERROR / mower offline na docker restart of mqtt_node kill: ESP-IDF MQTT layer raakt vast, kill+respawn lost het NIET op. FIX: ssh root@<mower-ip> 'bash /root/novabot/scripts/set_server_urls.sh' — clear stuck state + reconnect. Gevalideerd 2026-05-03 op LFIN1231000211 (Alain). Symptomen: mqtt_node alive maar geen TCP naar broker:1883, sensors map alleen cached settings (defaultCuttingHeight etc.) zonder battery_state/work_status, server toont online:true door PINGREQ van extended_commands.py maar zonder report_state_robot.
